description: Configures Okta Identity Governance separation of duties (SoD) policies. SoD prevents users from holding incompatible entitlements that create fraud or compliance risk (e.g., a user who can both create purchase orders and approve payments). Supports warning-only, hard block, and block-with-override enforcement modes.
terraform_support_notes: SoD policy shell via okta_policy. Rule documentation via okta_group. Toxic combination monitoring via okta_event_hook (requires custom endpoint). Actual SoD enforcement at request time, violation blocking, and override routing require the OIG Governance API (v2024.06+) or admin console.

risk_profile_effects.standard: SoD policies enabled in warn-only mode. Violations generate alerts but do not block access. No toxic combination detection. Advisory posture for initial SoD rollout.
risk_profile_effects.elevated: SoD violations blocked with override option. Toxic combination detection enabled. Critical conflicts blocked; overrides require app owner approval.
risk_profile_effects.strict: All SoD violations hard-blocked. No overrides. Toxic combination detection active. Zero tolerance for incompatible entitlements.

risk_profile.label: Risk Profile
risk_profile.client_description: Controls SoD enforcement strictness and toxic combination detection.

enable_sod_policies.label: Enable SoD Policies
enable_sod_policies.client_description: Enable separation of duties policy enforcement. When enabled, the system evaluates access requests against SoD rules before granting access.

sod_violation_action.label: Violation Action
sod_violation_action.client_description: How the system handles SoD violations. 'warn' shows a warning but allows the request to proceed (good for initial rollout). 'block_with_override' prevents the request but allows a senior approver to override. 'block' prevents the request with no override possible.

enable_toxic_combination_detection.label: Enable Toxic Combination Detection
enable_toxic_combination_detection.client_description: Proactively scan for toxic combinations of entitlements across users. Goes beyond individual request-time checks to find existing violations that accumulated before SoD policies were in place. Essential for initial SoD deployment to find pre-existing conflicts.

sod_rules.label: SoD Rules
sod_rules.client_description: Define incompatible entitlement pairs. Each rule names two entitlements that a single user should not hold simultaneously. Common examples: 'Create Purchase Order' + 'Approve Purchase Order', 'Manage Users' + 'Manage Audit Logs', 'Deploy to Production' + 'Approve Production Deploy'.

outputs.sod_policy_id: SoD enforcement policy ID. Null if disabled.
outputs.toxic_combination_detection_enabled: Whether toxic combination detection is active.
outputs.sod_rule_group_ids: Map of SoD rule names to documentation group IDs.
outputs.toxic_combination_hook_id: Toxic combination monitor hook ID. Null if disabled.
